---
title: "References"
format: pdf
---

These are the data sources, reports, and reference materials used throughout. 

## Data Sources

- Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED). *Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis.*  
  https://fred.stlouisfed.org/

- National Database of Childcare Prices. *U.S. Department of Labor / Child Care Aware of America.*  
  https://www.childcarecost.org/

- U.S. Census Bureau. *Historical Income Tables.*  
  https://www.census.gov/data/tables/time-series/demo/income-poverty/historical-income-households.html

- [Bureau of Labor Statistics — Consumer Expenditure Survey (CE) Household Spending Profiles](https://www.bls.gov/cex/cecomparison/ce_profile.htm)

- Economic Policy Institute. *State of Working America Wage Data.*  
  https://www.epi.org/

---

## Research & Reports

- [World Economic Forum — Charted: Here’s how US goods and services have changed in price since 2000](https://www.weforum.org/stories/2023/02/charted-heres-how-us-goods-and-services-have-changed-in-price-since-2000/)

- [Economic Policy Institute — America's Slow-Motion Wage Crisis: Four Decades of Slow and Unequal Growth](https://www.epi.org/publication/americas-slow-motion-wage-crisis-four-decades-of-slow-and-unequal-growth-2/)

- [Build, Baby, Build (Terner Center / Housing affordability report)](https://ti.org/pdfs/BuildBabyBuild.pdf)
